growth_records.cite: Kent et al. Increased biofilm formation due to high temperature adaptation in marine Roseobacter. Nature Microbiology (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41564-018-0213-8. [PMC6119078]
growth_records.snippet: Roseovarius sp. TM1035 ... optimally grows at 25°C at a rate of 0.15 OD600 h−1 (Supplementary Fig. 3).
growth_records.notes: Value stated verbatim in Methods as the ancestral strain's optimal growth rate. Units given by the authors are OD600 h-1 (rate of optical-density increase), not necessarily a per-capita exponential specific growth rate (mu, h-1); it may represent the maximal slope of the batch growth curve rather than true mu. Reported as-is with low confidence due to this unit ambiguity; no conversion to mu was attempted since the underlying OD time-course values were not given numerically in the text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]
